text:
This is super fun but I honestly prefer when Spencer has played the game before that way they don’t miss nearly as much! 

I don’t even blame them lol. Spencer is trying to explore the game, do bits, and keep Angela on track. I just feel like whenever we have these “showing our coworkers video games” vids we get a lot more out of it when the person is really familiar with the game, or at least has watched someone else play it before. 

This one actually didn’t bother me as much since I’m not very familiar with this game but with other instances when I am  these videos leave me STRESSED so I can definitely empathize with some of the people in the comments

author: @yokawa17
text: Nobody has as much passion as vico out of all the pros. It is really important that he won for fortnite competitive because he is someone who shows that he really cares about the game and takes it seriously.That will attract more new players and different kind of players. He is really good for the media and inspiring for upcoming pros to show passion and emotion. Since Kids look up to the best player, it is also nice to have a proper role model now. Alot of the pros are crying when something doesn't work out for them. Many of them say that they are done or want to quit the game every 5th sentence, or say its boring and so on. Of course the reason for that is that fortnite is just a really young esport and thats why the players are not that mature. But maybe vico will inspire more people to show proper sportsmanship and that will make the game bigger. The game becomes harder and crybabies are starting falling behind. This LAN showes that playing the game alot won't be enough in the future. Additionally it becomes more important that you have the will and the passion for it. I'm excited to see how fortnite comp will evolve.

text: Aphmau I want to tell you from the bottom of my heart how much your videos have helped me through some of the hardest moments in my life and how your stories your characters your energy and your kindness have given me a place where I could breathe again when everything around me felt heavy and confusing and I felt like I was sinking but every time I watched you play laugh create and bring joy into the world it felt like a light was shining through the darkness and reminding me that I am not alone and that happiness is still something I can feel and your videos became a safe place for me a place where I could escape the sadness for a little while and remember that there are still things worth smiling about and you helped me more than you will ever know because when I felt like giving up your voice and your world pulled me back and reminded me that I matter and that things can get better and I am so grateful for everything you have done without even knowing it and I hope you always understand how much your work means to people like me who needed someone to lift them up when they could not lift themselves

author: @fishermansdream36
text:
I remember when I used to watch you have the trap bases and instead of transferring all the loot to another base to where somebody can take it from you you set up a device to where you could suck out the loot and have a travel to a secondary base without having to travel with it.
But if you wanna have the base the secondary base far enough away that they don’t suspect that those are both your bases.
Then you would just have to chance it and make some trips back-and-forth with the most valuable loot.
But after watching you do that I started playing that way.
And it worked great always to have a secondary base. I don’t make one base too big because I gotta make another base so I make two decent sized basis sometimes I’ll build even a third or fourth base because of the first base was loaded. I got all my loot in the second base, but after I see the first base get loaded now I start building a third base in case somebody discovers one of my last two bases and whichever one they discover I just transfer all the loot to the one that they don’t know of and then I’m build another base that way I got multiple bases at least two going at the same time so anytime somebody looks like they’re gonna raid me. I can easily just move everything a value into the next base and they could spend all their effort and satchels and anything they use to loot my base where they could just waste it all for nothing and that sometimes is the most fun is watching them raid an empty base or a base where they’re just gonna get some crappy stuff. All the good stuff is gone. It’s in my other base and the scrap as soon as you get it you instantly use it so you never ever have too much scrap on you. It’s always been used to unlock something so no matter what even if you lost all your bases all your stuff everything you unlocked is still there. All you have to do is start a new base and you could start instantly buying everything again because they’re already unlocked. You don’t have to collect a whole bunch of scrap to do it again..
video_title: When a solo farmer angers the wrong Clan in Rust.. Ft oilrats and Qaixx

author: @Tenchigumi
text:
I have no idea how I missed your previous Half-Life video; I swear I didn't get an notification until THIS video popped up. Anyway, marathoning these two should make my weekend OT more bearable. 

Like death and taxes, sequels to profitiable new IPs are something we can't escape from, but we can at least hope that they'll lead to something better. That said, my fingers have been crossed for Half-Life 3 so long that they're basically a caduceus now. 

While I certainly enjoyed Half-Life 2's gameplay, presentation and story when I first played it decades ago, I still remember it more by its technical achievements than the game itself. Gaming magazines like Maximum PC lauded its groundbreaking usage of Direct X9, with one of its examples being their usage of shaders that ensured that water along shorelines appeared seamlessly transparent, rather than the stark polygonal outlines we'd seen in nearly every 3D game up to that point (the latter being a problem that persisted in AAA games as late as Skyrim). I also remember the game running far smoother than World of Warcraft (which released the same year), as I was able to finish HL2 on a somewhat budget PC, but WOW forced me to build a gaming PC. Looking back, HL2 achieved a feat PC games rarely accomplished: presenting something that technically appeared far ahead of its time, but performed admirably on hardware well before its time.

Man, I totally forgot about the wild legal battles between Vivendi and Valve (and probably didn't understand it all too well back then). Considering what I know now about corporate America, having internal memos outright stating orders to destroy records is unfathomably reckless; anyone who's suffered through corporate compliance training knows this. And it's even more baffling that Vivendi thought a mere Korean language barrier would be enough to shield them; considering that they were based in Los Angeles (a region that is home to one of the largest Asian populations outside of Asia itself) they really should have known better. Furthermore, Valve is based in Washington, which has a solid Asian population of its own. Altogether, the odds of a Korean speaker being present between the two companies (or available for legal counsel) was _incredibly_ high. Valve managed to dodge a bullet simply because Vivendi held the gun backwards.
video_title: Masterpiece or Madness? - Half Life 2 & Episodes Retrospective & History

author: @stevethepocket
text:
To me, it's not just the death of the _Half-Life_ series that pisses me off. It's the death of Valve as a developer of original games, and as an ever-growing supergroup of small dev teams who can push the medium forward. With the exception of _Half-Life_ itself and _Ricochet,_ their entire catalog only exists because of outside teams they pulled in, added to their staff, and gave the resources to produce a fleshed-out title or two—not just resources in the sense of money, an engine and unlimited time to finish, but direct access to the engine's developers and anyone else who's interested enough in the project to lend their expertise. (And both the original _Portal_ team and the original _Left 4 Dead_ team would learn the hard way how important that support was.)

While Valve does still hire people, their approach has shifted from hiring new devs who have fresh ideas but are in need of guidance to hiring industry veterans looking to pursue their passion in a more relaxed environment. Unfortunately I think this is what has led to what many have reported, that the reason new games almost never get made there anymore is that every individual there wants to work on their own project and they can't ever pull together big enough teams to get one past the concept stage. Gabe may be motivated by profitability, but he's not forcing anyone to only work on the most profitable projects. Rather, it's the every-man-for-himself structure that's leading to loads of squandered potential. You can see it in how even their most profitable games get very little work actually done on them, outside of whichever one is currently in its pre-release state.
video_title: Masterpiece or Madness? - Half Life 2 & Episodes Retrospective & History

author: @nerdoutreachprogram5358
text: The current generation of gamers will never know the absolutely mind blowing feeling of seeing footage from a new game whose graphics are exponentially better than the previous game. I mean throughout the 90s and 2000s we got to see so many literal technological breakthroughs. Starting with Quake in 1995, goddamn we thought that game looked _AMAZING_ and it did, then a significant improvement with Quake 2, Quake 3, and you remember the game Unreal? That was shocking to see it for the first time. Then Half Life obliterated every single expectation we had, did things nobody could imagine. Games nowadays are often excellent don’t get me wrong, but when I look forward to a new game or sequel it’s mostly for story and gameplay, and less about what technological breakthroughs the game will bring to the table, because games already look so fucking good. Getting a sequel today in which the graphics are 10-20% better looking can’t compare to what we felt going from Quake to Half Life, then Half Life 2 and so on.

author: @thetoyyya6890
text:
While I don't totally disagree with your conclusion it is worth noting that Valve have absolutely been working on and sinking a lot of resources into what in the code of the source 2 engine updates in other games is referred to as HLX in recent years. 

According to all signs in those source 2 updates it seems pretty far along in development and is not a VR game but instead seems to be a more standard 1st person shooter. In interviews after the release of Half life Alyx Valve also said directly that a big part of the reason for them making Alyx was to get over that mental hurdle of making Half Life games again. Clearly indicating that they want to make more than just Half Life Alyx, they also a while back went on a big hiring spree (by Valve standards) and the vast majority of those hired were Video Game devs so they are not just related to Valve's new hardware.

The voice Actor of the G-Man also teased that his role as the G-Man would make a return and we would hear more about it in 2025, now obviously we didn't get any kind of announcement in 2025 but it is likely that the game just got delayed and the dev team needed more time.

So overall there is actually very good reason to think that Valve have been working on Half Life 3 recently, that it's pretty far along in development and assuming no Valve shenanigans happen will most likely get announced somewhat soon. 

However it is Valve so those aforementioned shenanigans absolutely can happen at any time and the whole project could just get scrapped out of nowhere, but so far we have no indication that it has happened yet.
video_title: Masterpiece or Madness? - Half Life 2 & Episodes Retrospective & History

author: @acidicbloom
text: It’s very rare that I actually watch a gaming video from start to finish without looking away. Your work is like proper journalism, without any unnecessary or pointless information. So, yet another thank you for your brilliant content. Actually, HL2 was the first game I played on my first PC back in 2007. And perhaps because my gaming experience was very limited, I grew to love this game so much, or maybe it’s simply its undeniable merit and significance to the industry that made me fall in love with it – it doesn’t really matter now. It’s very rare that I feel like saying a project is true art, and this is one of those moments when I’ll say it without a doubt. It’s one of those games that shapes players’ tastes and consciousness. I hope we haven’t gone mad and that Valve will do it a third time. I want to believe it.

author: @linksora7120
text:
I love how ancient and strange the regis all feel, they have always been my favourite legendary trio in gen 3 and my favourite group of legendaries overall. Regice is my favourite because of its thunderbolt ice beam combination as well as it’s insane special defence and I’m happy to see it provide a useful niche here with that curse idea

Also I love the idea of this team as:
Regigigas the supportive father 
Regirock as the little diva girl throwing tantrums (the name diva is hilarious) swinging her handbag around and being a menace. 
Regice is the quiet sibling protecting their family and becoming angry when they need to, cursing out others when they need to but chilling in the back  until then. 
Registeel is the cool brother just untouchable by force and comes out swinging over time. 
Regieleki is the cute little child jumping around full of life and joy and just happy to be here. 
And regidrago is the oldest of the siblings absolutely fierce and terrifying always ready to fight. 

A dad, their cute child, their diva and the three brothers cool angry and quiet

author: @Kuzibabapirogaliba
text:
Cookie recepy 

1. Creaming the DoughPrep the Pans: If you do not have a specific 12x3-inch long tart/bar pan, fold heavy-duty aluminum foil to create two 12x3-inch rectangular "boats" on a baking sheet. Line them with parchment paper.Butter & Sugar: In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, brown sugar, and white sugar with a mixer on medium-high speed for 2 minutes until creamy and fluffy.Wet Ingredients: Add the egg and vanilla extract. Beat until fully incorporated and smooth.2. Mixing & ChillingDry Ingredients: Add the baking soda and salt. Gradually add the flour, mixing on low speed just until the flour disappears. Do not overmix, or the cookie will become tough.Chocolate: Reserve 2 tablespoons of chocolate chips for the top. Fold the remaining chips into the dough using a spatula.Chill (Crucial Step): Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. Chilling prevents the long cookie from spreading into a flat pancake.3. Shaping & BakingPreheat: Turn your oven to 350°F (175°C).Shape: Divide the chilled dough into two equal portions. Press each portion into the bottom of your prepared long pans, shaping them into even 12-inch logs.Bake: Bake for 20 to 23 minutes. The edges should be golden brown, but the center must still look slightly soft and underbaked.Cool: Press the reserved chocolate chips into the top of the hot cookies immediately after taking them out of the oven. Let them cool completely in the pan for 1 hour. Moving them while warm will cause the long structure to break

author: @vichasfunlol
text:
Asking for an Among us mod part 7 (I think)

If Ssundee actually sees this comment, I recommend making a mod about the movie "The Black Phone." There are two movies in the series, so the map will be fairly decent in size. For an imposter transportation item, I recommend ice skates (which could also be used to eliminate a player) for the snow area and the black van for the regular area. For crewmates, I recommend the transportation item being Billy Showalter's bike, which can be attained by completing the quest that he gives. 

Here are some suggestions for the quests.

Robin Arelleone, quest- Fight Moose. 
Ability for crewmates- stun other players with a punch. 
Ability for imposter- Eliminate a player with a punch (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Billy Showalter, quest- Deliver six newspapers
Ability for crewmates- Billy's bike for transportation
Ability for imposter-Billy's bike for transportation and running over players (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Griffin Stagg, quest- Protect him against a three-wave attack from bullies 
Ability for crewmates- Call Griffin to temporarily protect them for 120 seconds
Ability for imposter- Call Griffin to eliminate a player of your choosing (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Vance Hopper, quest- Beat him in pinball
Ability for crewmates- Summons a pinball machine that could be used to hide from other players.
Ability for imposter- Summons a pinball machine to crush players (multiple uses with a 100 second cooldown)

Bruce Yamada, quest- Hit a baseball farther than he can
Ability for crewmates- Temporarily has a baseball gold medal that deflects any attacks onto a nearby crewmate.
Ability for imposter- Can turn the gold medal giant and roll it across the map, eliminating the first two players it hits (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Finney Blake, quest- Help him find his rocket flashlight.
Ability for crewmates- Temporarily see in the dark with a flashlight when lights go out.
Ability for imposter- Disintegrate whoever is in the flashlight ray, first two players it reaches permanently eliminated (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Felix, quest- Find his remains in the frozen lake in the snow area.
Ability for crewmates- Call Felix's ghost to halt the imposter form moving for thirty seconds.
Ability for imposter- Call Felix's ghost to jump scare the selected player, causing disorientation, hallucinations, and possibly blinding them (multiple uses on a 120 second cooldown)

Cal, quest- Find his remains in the supply shed close to the frozen lake
Ability for crewmates- Gives players an ax to temporarily stun the imposter.
Ability for imposter- Use ax to cut someone's face in half on a tree stump (one time, afterwards only blinds players)

Spike, quest- Find the remains of his jacket in the furnace of the food hall in the snow area.
Ability for crewmates- Summons a jacket that protects them from attacks for thirty seconds (120 second cooldown)
Ability for imposter- Eliminate a player by wrapping them in the jacket, causing a heatstroke (multiple uses, long cooldown)

Their can also be a crewmate role which I recommend should be Gwendolyn (Gwen) Blake, after completing all their tasks, they get a dream that reveals who's the imposter. So it'll lead to a challenge where the imposter has to figure out who is the Gwen role.


For the imposter skin, I recommend it being the Grabber due to the fact he's the main villain in both movies. If Ssundee and his team actually make this a mod for a video, I would deeply appreciate it

You go back to it at a certain point but in a different part, and you have to avoide a giant spider that is actively searching for people to kill, but can't get inside hotel rooms, where the three required TV trolleys are at, and Level 37 makes a return as Level 37.2, and Level Fun makes a return as Level Fun+, but if you want a quick ending, go for either the Suburbs one or the Pink House one, the Suburbs one requiring you to get to Level 922 and the Pink House one requiring you to get to Level 974, 974 having one singular entity called Mr Kitty that ends up putting you to sleep with knock out gas, and when you wake up, 974 looks clean and restored, and Mr Kitty is more friendly, and every ending leads to the Bunker, which leads to the Overgrowth, the origin of the Bacteria.

author: @tedraymondLP
text:
I’m happy Arin addressed some of the comments on this series. 

I’ve been having a blast with this run, and it’s been funny and interesting to watch. It may not be a “competent romp,” as Arin said, but that’s what makes it compelling! I hope the boys keep this up even longer and I’m also glad it seems to be doing well, by Arin’s own admission, viewswise. Thanks @GameGrumps! 

Here’s where the Grumplocke stands so far: 
Gym badges: 5
Available Pokémon: 5 in team, 6 in box (+1 Buneary for Amity Square) - Arin is headed to get a Riolu egg. 
Dead Pokémon: 10 
Dan's quiz score: 30%

Arin’s most recent catch was in Ep. 27: Greg the Gastly in Lost Tower. 
Arin’s most recent death was in Ep. 23: Bob the Floatzel to a Graveler’s self-destruct.

He has not caught anything on the following routes: 205, 208, 209*, 210, 212, 213, 214, 215, 218, 219, -220, and 221- (e: hasn't been here yet). He’s also not caught anything in Ravaged Path, Floaroma Meadow, the Old Chateau, the Ruin Maniac’s Cave, the Trophy Garden, nor has he fished in any towns with water. The gift Porygon remains unclaimed in Veilstone City. (*Some nuzlockes consider Lost Tower and Route 209 to be the same location.) Lots of options available! 

I guess we're facing Byron next week! How will Arin handle the Steel-type gym? He's leveled-up and has options, but there could be risk!

To be continued ⬅

As someone who didn’t like the film, a bigger problem in my opinion was how painfully slow it felt. At moments it appeared like the film was dragging its shoes through the mud without any good result at the end to excuse the wait.

The film was also really confusing due to how disconnected it felt from what was already created. I understand it’s his take on the backrooms but it didn’t feel like the backrooms if that’s understandable. Wasn’t expecting any Roblox or Fortnite or whatever from it but at least some of what had already been done of the backrooms ya know?

Lastly, I find another issue to be the entity confusion. Is the entity from the start the same as the one we see at the end? Is the entity that appears when Kat is taking about being behind a mirror the same as the one from the start or end? Why is the entity the pirate guy? Why does the pirate bleed but the other entities don’t? It’s all very confusing.

I don’t know why people would expect Roblox Backrooms though.

author: @watermelonprom7197
text:
I went to see the movie in theaters & I can confirm this is an actual thing 😭😅

The movie itself was pretty great :] I have a personal gripe with it due to the fact that I don't enjoy how metaphor heavy horror movies have recently become. 

It's to the point that you could literally make the argument that the entire movie was just the result of the main characters having a mid life crisis & a mental breakdown. Implying that none of it was actually real & that it was all just a psychotic episode. 

For me personally I don't mind the metaphors, infact I actually quite enjoy them on an intellectual level. However, I find the whole "was it even real" angle as such a boring cliche that is only really good when it is the main focus of the movie. Otherwise it may lead to the movie sacrificing certain aspects of itself inorder to make the metaphor work. It may also lead to the viewer disassociating from the experience & asking themselves "what was even the point". 

Which is why I believe many "fans" took such a negative orientation towards this film. It annoys me personally because why can't we just have a movie that is able to stand on its own? It's like watching a giant monster movie, nobody gives a fuck about the humans & their social dynamics. They just wanna see the giant monster! In the same vain, I wish that many horror movies would just focus less on the Metaphor behind it all & more on the actual horror itself.

However, in Kane Parson's defense, I believe that focusing on the metaphor was more about proving to both the audience, the critics & himself that he could make an astoundingly deep horror movie. With that being said, I do believe the movie suffered from this decision. It was forced to shift focus in the second act to fully flesh out said metaphor. Which ended up making the ending feel disconnected & coming out of nowhere. Which was another main gripe that many "fans" had. 

Overall though the movie was fantastic :> The horror was surprisingly grounded for how Sci-Fi the whole film was. Which is thanks to the movie's metaphor & the actual nature of The Backrooms as a horror genre itself. For what it was, it did very well. Though I know that I & other individuals would have preferred some different content I suppose. 

Now, as for the individuals who say that the film itself was bad. I have but one thing to ask. 

What was the metaphor?

In order to be able to give a proper critique & analysis of the film, you actual have to understand the message that the movie was trying to tell. To be able to say wether or not it has objectively achieved what it was striving for. 

Otherwise, your words are just meaningless trash that should be swept away & into the garbage.

Though in my opinion, most are just saying that it's trash just to grab the attention of others. They shall soon forget the movie as we shall soon forget them.
video_title: The Backrooms Movie Disaster

author: @JohnWisks
text:
The Backrooms was good, but it missed being a masterpiece by focusing too much on the corporate/therapist storyline instead of Clark’s descent into madness.
Cutting away to the therapist right when Clark's exploration peaked completely killed the momentum. The therapist's side should’ve just been quick flashbacks. We needed to be trapped in that endless maze with Clark as his mind spiraled, using the Backrooms as a metaphor for escaping his divorce.
Plus, the lore about the Backrooms being made of corrupted memories is incredible. It’s like asking someone to draw a dog when they've never actually seen one the rooms try to recreate reality, but the details are completely wrong and glitched.
That’s why the red-haired entity with 3 faces was definitely his wife; the maze "forgot" what she looked like, and her running away from him mirrors her kicking him out in real life. Even crazier, the main entity that kills his interns is a corrupted manifestation of Clark himself. i felt it was fucked up that he didn't tell his interns that something was down there and which led to them dying. The ultimate tragedy is that this double only kills Clark at the very end because the therapist convinces him to let go of his baggage and reality leaving him completely defenseless to his own inner demons. That psychological angle deserved way more focus than the therapist stuff. which i felt in all honesty couldve been done in the 2nd film if they have one. its still a good movie its just thats where i feel the movie did too much for what little time it had and its mainly because of the therapists flashbacks and her scenes. it still a good movie i give it a 6.5 out of 10.
video_title: The Backrooms Movie Disaster

author: @iwong3357
text: The funny thing is, Scar had all the resources to mop up the easy way with sponges...  They can't dry out entire pools, but they can dry out smaller columns of water from the top down, or even just a narrow hallway starting from on end at the top.  He would have saved himself a lot of trouble by using a fraction of that sand to cut up the water into smaller columns and using the sponges to dry them out one by one so they can't refill each other like they can in a massive open pool.  Cub will probably get a kick out of that upon watching this video.

author: @jingl4060
text:
Here’s a recipe for a cake 

Baked Oreo Cake

Oreo

Ingredients

* 24 Oreo cookies
* 240 ml milk
* 1 tsp baking powder
* Optional:
    * 2 eggs (for a fluffier cake)
    * 1 tsp vanilla extract
    * Extra crushed Oreos for topping

Instructions

1. Preheat the oven
    * 180°C (160°C fan-forced).
2. Make the batter
    * Blend the Oreos into fine crumbs.
    * Add milk and baking powder.
    * If using eggs and vanilla, add them now.
    * Blend until smooth.
3. Prepare the pan
    * Grease an 18–20 cm cake tin or line it with baking paper.
4. Bake
    * Pour the batter into the tin.
    * Sprinkle extra crushed Oreos on top if desired.
    * Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until a skewer inserted in the center comes out mostly clean.
5. Cool
    * Let the cake cool in the tin for 10 minutes.
    * Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

Optional Oreo Frosting

Mix:

* 250 ml whipped cream
* 4 crushed Oreos

Spread over the cooled cake and decorate with whole Oreos.

author: @hampter_double0100
text:
About the Day system in this game: The Day symbols are meant to be The Seven Deadly Sins. Each of the core characters of those specific days representing the sins. 

These are:
Bishop > Purple > Pride
Astrologist > Blue > Sloth
Inquisitor > Red > Wrath
Ms. Charm > Pink > Lust
Snake > Green > Envy
The Merchant > Orange > Gluttony 
Now, there are 6 sins and days here. Which leaves Greed. That is you. You, the player represent Greed. 
I certainly think that this game could use some rework at some points, but this little part of the lore is very cool to me.

author: @Snickersnek
text:
Fun fact, "Bird type" WAS a thing in Gen 1! While it was replaced with "Flying" type, Missingno is Bird/Normal, the only instance of a true Bird-type pokémon. So at least for a while in development, it would seem that "Bird" was considered a type as much as Bug and Dragon. 
With that in mind, Bird-keepers and the Gym tutor NPC mentioning the bird-type makes a lot more sense.

author: @zozzlez
text:
I’m so excited to see you play this RT!!!! Fire Red was my very first Pokemon game and what introduced me to and made me come to love the franchise. 

I shared a save with my older sister and we would play together. Not because we only had one game mind you, I _technically_ had my own copy of leaf green (benefits of having two kids is getting both editions!), but because I couldn’t quite _read_ well enough to play independently yet (<- born in 2001 for reference, i don’t remember what year exactly we were given the games fwiw). So we played together, and Pokemon helped me learn how to read :]

I remember as a child us taking on the elite 4 and struggling and _struggling_ til we FINALLY managed to beat Lance, barely any HP or PP left, only to discover that there was a _5th battle_ with the champion, your _rival._ We never managed to win and finally beat our rival, and I remember being frustrated by it at the time.

….Revisiting the save as an _adult,_ there’s only three Pokemon even CLOSE to leveled high enough for the E4 (the rest around mid 20s) and it was a Moltres, a Charizard, and a _second_ Charizard. The children YEARN for the fire/flying type (they were the Cool Strong Ones™ in our child brains). They all had a bunch of 5/5 moves (larger damage number means better attack no matter what 👍) and no type coverage at all. One of the charizards still had _ember._

unfortunately to this day I do remain somewhat part of The Problem in that I’m the _exact_ target market of the charizard pandering they do. what did FLAMEZ and Fireman (real names) ever do to you 🥺

… gosh this comment got longer than intended. thanks for the indulging my stroll down memory lane there BFSMSN. maybe this vid can be the nostalgia fuel I need to finally dig out the old cartridge and finally make/grind up a proper team and beat it for childhood me ❤
video_title: Let's Play All of Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen

author: @floppydisksareop
text: It's such a pleasure to see you play this, since this was my first pokemon game ever. Not because I'm old, but because I never had a console and could only emulate this one at the time. Still, it's been almost a decade even then, so does make the memories come flooding back. I fell in love with the series then, and then it was further cemented by you shiny hunting for Cupcake. I was just starting high school back then, I'm almost done with my master's now. It feels so distant and yet so recent. It's so crazy to look back and remember some of the happy moments I had back at the time, despite being generally quite miserable at the time. Thank you for the many years of happy memories that got me through rough times, and thanks for making me reminisce a little bit about something else that also helped me at some point in my life.

author: @cwsojourner
text: I'm an Old Gamer (played Pong and Space Invaders on Atari as a kid...), and I can tell you for a fact that games used to be released in playable form 99.9% of the time. This was simply because there was no convenient way for updates to reach the consumer. So, I know it's possible. The executives at gaming company, most of whom are from Business School and not Coding School, got f-ing complacent and lazy when the Internet became a thing. While it's true that games have scaled up exponentially in terms of complexity and sheer volume of code, so has the the number of people working on the games and the size of the budgets for the games. I'd buy pre-releases of my favorite titles if I knew they were going to work they way they should the first time. But I don't like being forced to be a Beta tester while paying full price. I have better things to do with my time and money, and I've learned patience. Screw 'em!

author: @jefferynighswander2410
text:
I lived in Japan for 12 years. You can't buy or find an X Box system or game ANYWHERE. Not even used. I broke a controller once and couldn't fine one for weeks until I found one in the COMPUTER section of Manga Souko. This was after I had asked two different employees. I took it to the counter and said "This is an X Box controller, you told me there were none, but this is one." It said X Box all over the box. It was an official controller for the system. The guy at the counter was baffled. He took the box from me and looked at it on all sides and then said "This is for X Box?" They didn't even know it was for X Box because they had never seen one. 

I am sure people would buy X Box in Japan, but they just don't sell them ANYWHERE. It's Nintendo, Sony or nothing.
video_title: 10 WEIRD Gaming Stories of May 2026

author: @walsh451
text:
Here in the UK the average new game went from £50 to £70 overnight. With everything else going up, wages generally stagnating, you have to consider far more what you spend your money on. I now wait for games to come down in price, for example I really enjoyed the BF6 beta, but was not going to pay full price, I only got it a few weeks ago on sale for £35. I have a list as long as my arm of games I am "waiting on" to come down in price more. I brought FIFA every year on release from 1998-2020, but the increase in Price was far too much. If games were still £50 I would buy more newer titles.

Add to that the fact that allot of games come out with problems that need patching, it only really makes sense to wait it out.

author: @alejandrotello7903
text:
I literally discovered this game at the beginning of the week, and I finished it yesterday, and DXTR just made a video about it XD.

The game is amazing for its attention to RNG, but even so, on night 6, it seems to me that Spring Bonnie's reaction time drops from about 3 seconds to just one second in the last hour.

And why the heck does an entrance to the vent give you an instant kill if you don't close it, but in the broken vent, it lets you put on the mask?

Besides, you have to prioritize Spring Bonnie when he enters the vents and Fredbear is attacking at the same time, compared to Bub, who deactivates when two of the four bastards are attacking you.

author: @IMaN1GHTM4R3
text: Hey so coupla things I noticed while playing for myself... Spring Bonnie will move faster and eventually ignore the audio lure all together if it's used too much (and similarly to Bub can't attack while Fredbear or Monty are). The devs planned for Fredbear and Monty attacking together yes, but you can actually still move your screen during Fredbear's attack, this makes it so much easier dealing with Fredbear and Monty together. And lastly, Bub will always move to cam 5 first and if they don't attack there, then he'll move to 4 and attack there guaranteed. Sry for being the paragraph guy but thought I'd give some tips for anyone wanting to play the game themselves.

author: @Krubbster
text:
I absolutely love the designs for these mobs! They perfectly capture the Pale Garden aesthetic while looking unique from each other and their inspirations. Syphoning is the most unique fanmade status effect I’ve seen and the first that really seems like it would make fighting a lot more dangerous. Great new uses for resin too.

I understand the modder’s point that the skeleton and zombie variants aren’t terribly revolutionary, so I tried to think of some additional mobs and I think I had some of the worst ideas ever:
 
Creaky Creeper: a pale wood and resin themed creeper that either gets slowed or immobilized when looked at, but has its speed and explosion empowered based on how long it was looked at.

• Could charge you despite being looked at when it reaches a certain power level. Maybe takes 10 or so seconds of staring to fully empower it. 

• For balancing the automatic charge could be incredibly fast but remove its ability to steer so you could potentially use it as a weapon against other mobs.

• It wouldn’t be terribly strong by itself, but when paired with other mobs, or God forbid it being protected by a Blooming Heart, it would make things way more challenging. 


Crawling Drainroot: Another supportive mob that exists as a source block with a tentacle-like entity extending from it. It can turn several times and go up and down hills to influence things a good distance from its source block. It could have various abilities like: 

• Tether to players, allowing them to move but preventing them from moving a certain distance away. 

• Prick things with resin thorns to briefly inflict Weakness.

• Lay in your path and act like a living fence block, essentially corralling you for other mobs to capitalize off of. Would pair nicely with the weakness thorns. A more vanilla version of this would be if it was just a normal plant block with collision.

• I’d like it if it could drain blocks of their color to spread the Pale Garden, essentially an analog of the Skulk Shrieker but I don’t know how to best implement that. Maybe it can create guaranteed spawn points for other minions?


Resin Leech: Idea of a bug-like creature that latches on and has some secondary effect would fit, but it seems like it would just encroach on the niche of the Syphon-inflicting mobs. 

Anyone feel free to tell me your thoughts!

author: @searchingforalexandra
text: Hey Jay, I just wanna say thank you for being a big part of my life I've been here since yandere sims videos. I went through many different stages of life and lost all consistency in all aspects but watching your videos for comfort sometimes even rewatching videos i already watched to sleep. You were a source of comfort for 15 years old me who stayed in the hospital for months on end struggling with ed, 17 years old me during my worst heartbreak, 18 years old me deep into drugs and alcohol, 19 years old me after running away from home to another state alone for 6 months working two jobs to afford rent and having to steal the supermarket downstairs' wifi to watch your new uploads, 20 years oId me after I broke off my engagement and cut him out my life due to physical abuse, and me now at 21 recovering from aborting an accidental pregnancy. You'll forever be a part of all my favorite memories, and maybe one day if I ever have kids I'll pass my love for you to them too maybe they'll also find the same comfort in you that I've seen for years. I wish you nothing but the best and I hope this community never dies because it's genuinely one of the best and most genuine communities out there. author: @InvaderShane
text:
The fact is whether Marathon was a success or not doesnt matter, it doesn't even matter if Marathon never existed at all.

Destiny 2 was always going to end at some point. I dont understand how people thought D2 would just live forever, its been 10 years. Once the Final Shape dropped the main story concluded.  Why continue playing? If Marathon didnt exist I still think we would have ended up at this point. There just wasn't enough people playing D2 to justify continued development because the interest dropped off dramatically after The Final Shape, not to mention how hard it is for new players to get into the game.  

Its like Endgame with the MCU. As soon as Endgame released and the massive 10 year story concluded myself and many others no longer cared about the MCU. The story I cared about was wrapped up, and it ended in a satisfying way. I got what I needed from the story. Almost everything that has released after Endgame has been terrible and a growing number of people just dont care like they once did. 

Destiny 2 is in the same boat. The majority of people found The Final Shape to be the perfect time to stop playing because the main story building up all these years is finished, anything outside of a sequel is going to feel pointless. 

I stopped playing D2 when they started vaulting content, I've never seen a studio do that. And you're right it completely alienated new players or even returning players from actually enjoying the game. I paid for that content, the fact I couldnt replay the main story again if I wanted is such a slap in the face. When you have concluded your games main story and make it impossible to gain and retain new players who is left to play? Only the most diehard players. 

D3 should have already been in development as Final Shape was nearing release even if it was just pre production and earlt stage planning. In fact if they were smart D3 could've been getting a reveal this year, they could've been 3+ years into development or something by this point and could've started talking about core features of the game and how it continues the world and all that stuff. The fact that Bungie has nothing now besides Marathon shows their incompetence and Sonys as well. Its crazy that Bungie botched one of the world's biggest games. 

Destiny 1 had a rocky life cycle especially early on because it was something almost entirely new. You can't blame them for having a hard time trying to find out exactly what Destiny was supposed to be. The first Destiny game really didnt find it footing until The Taken King and the fact they made a lot of the same mistakes with D2 is so strange. 

The fact is Marathon is never going to be the success that Sony wants it to be. And if Sony refuse to let Bungie begin work on D3 I really dont see a future where Bungie to continues to exist as they do today.
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @ChannelKit
text:
How was Destiny 2's pricing anything other than awful? We had expansions, season passes, dungeon keys, event cards, premium cosmetics, character boosts, and even transmog was originally designed around monetization. Some armor sets were $20+ per character, and crossover cosmetics pushed prices even higher.

On top of that, Bungie regularly priced Silver packs so they didn't match item costs, meaning you often had to buy the next tier up just to get enough currency. Destiny 2 didn't just have microtransactions. It stacked monetization systems on top of monetization systems.

author: @Achilles3602
text: I think a big problem about theoretically doing like a "soft relaunch" of destiny 2, with all the new stuff mentioned, new onboarding, etc. is that even then the game has too much baggage, and more importantly it has too much anger. Sunsetting was an awful idea, but players will (arguably justifiably) never ever let it go, any discussion about the game is forever tainted by it (some refer to the big vaulting circa beyond light, some only refer to seasons rotating out, one is more fixable than the other). The player base is also so divided, Bungie at their peak could not fill the chomping maws of both the PVP and PVE player bases, both of which are loud, demanding and highly entrenched in why they play destiny, a smaller Bungie couldn't manage it and the resulting portions of the player base inevitably left wanting would instantly be mad again, tainting any hope of the game getting a fresh start. There's also no trust in Bungie I feel, why invest your time in something that may or may not be bungled so fantastically again like we know Bungie has in the past?
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @RayearthIX
text:
The issue with Destiny 2 is that baggage. I played it at launch and on and off for a few years. Every time I came back to Destiny 2, the experience was worse. More content was vaulted, more seasonal crap was shoved in my face potentially making whatever I had meaningless, and I just had issues figuring out what to even do when playing. The game had an abysmal returning player experience to go with its even worse new player experience. That was an obvious problem for YEARS and they never fixed it and I stopped playing the game because it felt like an irregular player like me was just wasting my time in a game that had no interest in my being there. Bungie’s inability and unwillingness to fix that issue is what killed the game in the long run. Sony’s decision is to basically back a new horse and hope it can grow into something instead of backing the old one that’s been fading for years.

That written… I would consider playing a Destiny 3 or return to D2 if they ever fixed the issues above. I have no interest whatsoever in Marathon.

author: @Sekiberius
text:
People don't want destiny 3 for better graphics, people want a Destiny 3 for an engine built from the ground up to support the kind of live service they wanted to support in D2.
Destiny 2's largest failing was that it was never originally developed with becoming a live service in mind, a decision that ended up causing more and more technical and development issues as time went on. Destiny 3 was the chance to wipe the slate clean, and ensure a strong and long lasting foundation that could withstand over a decades worth of content without needing to gut what came before.

text:
Hard disagree on D3 not helping this franchise. How are you gonna seriously state that when it's practically confirmed at this point D2 is built on some of the worst spaghetti code known to man? author: @KhaoskillR
text:
Certified Marathon enjoyer and I didn't agree with some of your points in your review of that game but I gotta say man, you hit the nail on the head with that destiny 2 segment. 

I love marathon. I love the promise of it's world, and the potential for experimentation it offers up. But I also think that that experimentation should be a side project for bungie. It should be funded in part through a thriving destiny 2 and allowed to build it's player base slowly and carefully rather than with all the pressure and expectation of being *the* bungie game.

I played destiny 1, I played destiny 2 when it came out, I played forsaken. I enjoyed all of these things. Then I stopped. Then I tried to come back, and I just... couldn't. I've tried several times over the past few years and nothing made sense, the story was disjointed, and everything felt like it had moved on without me. Why on earth would I bother to invest the time?

All they had to do was spend a bit of money, resource and time doing a soft reboot of Destiny 2 to bring people back in, round out the story issues, and make it feel like a complete package. 

Something I've come to realise is that when I have a bright idea - like, for example, "I should go back and try destiny 2 again" - I'm not special and I'm usually not the only one having that thought; far from it. I'm 100% sure loads of people like me over the years have tried going back to destiny, with such an enticing world and such tight gameplay, and bounced off it like a water droplet off oil. 

It's truly a tragedy to see such potential squandered so needlessly. I've never nodded and said "yes" so many times watching one of your videos as I have watching this one. 

What a complete and utter shame. There's no other way to describe it.

author: @keiganblaise9878
text:
To be honest, we don't even need new story content. If they could just give us more playable, explorable PvE spaces, more Strikes, and more PvP and Gambit maps, I think that would go a LONG way in helping people find interest in Destiny again. I think one of the biggest, if not THE biggest problem with Destiny 2, is the fact that so much content has just been missing from the game, for so long. Both past content that we PAID FOR literally taken out of the game (Red War campaign, first two DLCs, Forsaken), and new content that we have been EXPECTING but unfortunately never came (PvP and Gambit maps, new worldspaces, new Strikes). Just give us raw, playable content. No story needs to be attached. If we could get Old Chicago, Old London, Old New York, more Venus, more Mars, more Mercury, more Titan, etc... We don't necessarily need a reason to go to those places except "the Vanguard has granted us authority to access these areas that were previously off-limits". Just the thought of exploring new places fills me with wonder. Give us Old Chicago and drop the Shattered Cycle story, at least for the foreseeable future. Give us Old London with no strings attached. Let us explore old areas from Destiny 1, updated for Destiny 2, like the Ishtar Collective on Venus, just because. Why not?

Maybe I'm wrong, and I have no idea what the majority of Destiny players really want. But that's my guess, and it's what _I_ want. I love the story, but I feel like the story is what takes the longest to get right and implement. If you guys could just create worldspaces and let us explore for the fuck of it, wouldn't that both lessen the workload AND please players?

I can't help but feel like the final update should include this. A bunch of new (or old D1) playable spaces and Strikes/PvP/Gambit maps, alongside a final broadcast from the Vanguard saying, "Guardians, all planets and old settlements are now a go. Set out, explore, gather intelligence, and reclaim the lost treasures of our Golden Age. You have our permission. Now get out there and make humanity proud."
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @BubbleTea033
text:
Just chiming in to 100% disagree with your thesis. I will not every buy a new piece of content within Destiny 2. If there's a three on the box, and the game isn't using the 25 year old Tiger Engine, I'm on board. Anything else is an absolute no-go. Destiny 2 does not have life left in it and I think you're framing the post Final Shape era incorrectly. Bungie did announce new content and promise us that the future was bright. They announced a whole new saga. No one cared because they don't want more Destiny 2. They are tired of this iteration, this formula, with all of this baggage and technical debt. I think my position is pretty common. I would not play any future Destiny 2 content that wasn't free. I simply reject the endless live-service model. It's not my job to care about the finances / business side of things. That's for the people with the MBAs to figure out.

You suggested that Marathon should have had a more transparent and open development, maybe even going the early access model. I think that's what D3 should do. Make a 2-3 hour campaign introduction, throw in a strike or two, and start with a limited number of classes and subclasses, not unlike D1's alpha or D2's beta. But within those limitations, show us a vision for the future of the franchise that feels new and exciting. Titans with exclusive weapon types that are too heavy for other classes. Hunter Golden Gun can now empower all weapon archetypes and they each work differently. Warlocks can study hive and taken magic, summon minion thralls and other crazy shit, etc. Maybe dual-wielding? Titans can dual wield smgs and lmgs, hunters can dual wield hand cannons and sidearms, warlocks can't dual-wield but if they use a one-handed weapon, their left hand is free to cast spells uninterrupted. Come up with something that feels completely fresh and shows that there's a future focused on deep build-crafting and then deliver content at a cadence that suggests to the community that more than one raid a year isn't impossible.

author: @CtrlAble
text:
What stood out to me most here is how much the game seems to understand the difference between playing as a spy and simply playing a shooter with a spy skin. A lot of action games tell you that you're a master infiltrator, but then every problem is solved the same way. What makes this look interesting is how many different systems reinforce the fantasy. 

Disguises, environmental interactions, social spaces, hacking tools, stealth routes, and improvised problem-solving all create situations where you're constantly making decisions instead of just clearing rooms. I also think it's funny that the video accidentally highlights an important aspect of player freedom. The game clearly gives players a wide range of options, and the immediately use that freedom to create absolute chaos. 

That's usually a good sign. Some of the most memorable games are the ones where serious systems and ridiculous player behavior can coexist. 

From a player experience perspective, the biggest compliment I can give is that every clip looked like a unique story rather than a repeated gameplay loop. Whether it's sneaking through a party, causing a distraction, chasing targets, or accidentally turning a mission into a disaster, the systems seem to create memorable moments instead of scripted ones. That's often what keeps games fun long after the credits roll.
video_title: 007 Fist Fight

author: @TWELVE-ax7
text: Keep in mind that your 4Seam, Cutter and Sinker can all be thrown through the same tunnel (thrown towards the same point from the same arm angle) and all of them will keep high velocity. 4Seam is "straight/up" Cutter breaks slightly to your glove-side, Sinker breaks slightly to your arm-side. Your off-speed (low velo) pitches are the Changeup and Freak-ball. The Changeup should break arm-side and your Freak-ball should break downward (like a curve/slider). Pick your tunnels against Righties and Lefties wisely, but always throw through tunnels, with velo or against velo depending on the count. If you start high-velo and get a strike, consider throwing a ball off-speed to test the waters and get a batter swinging. The opposite can be true. rant over. have fun!

author: @BigBeardOne
text:
To put into perspective how hard the immaculate inning is, the closest anyone has come to a 9 swinging strike immaculate inning (all 9 pitches resulted in missed swings and no fouls) is 8 last year by Mason Miller. 

As far as I've ever read, nobody has ever thrown 9 swinging strikes in a single inning. You have to have perfect control, know every batter's tendencies, and put the fear of God into the other team. 

Another weird stat is most strikes looking in an immaculate inning is 6, which was done by two different dudes.

An I-I is just 9 strikes by any means. Fouls, looks, whiffs. Iirc, it's happened 130 times. /Tism

text: I can't wait for when all of these updates roll out and a few years (maybe months, I can't tell for sure) down the road, the corporation starts questioning why people don't play anymore. ——

THE UNIMPORTANT ONES

Tobias Stolz-Zwilling, Communications Director

THE IMPORTANT ONES

Joanna Nowak – Historical Consultant

——

author: @spoof4957
text: Hydrostatic pressure wouldn't make the water squirt out of a fountain to the same altitude of a body of water. It would just squirt at a greater pressure the higher the altitude difference is, because gravity is causing the psi to rise in a pressurized system. The water will only rise to the same level, assuming it's fully contained (like in a pipe), and extending above the altitude of the body of water. If the pipe was closed off at the end, it actually wouldn't even push up to the same altitude of the body of water, because air pressure is also trapped inside, blocking the water from rising to the same altitude. The water won't come all the way up unless you then open the top of he pipe, which is called venting the pipe. If it was a large enough system that was filled with water, you would feel the air shoot out of the pipe if you opened it up, kinda similar to the reaction of a hole shooting the pressure out of a rocket ship into space. Don't know why I felt the need to share this, but I did.

author: @tomasdowner
text:
Use restoration potion glitch. 
*Add +2000% sneak to be invisible even while casting spells on enemies. Bump into them, they never see you period. Only if you stand up. Dragons can't find you. Facts. It can be problematic lol.
*Add +2000% lockpicking to open ANY lock without even spinning them. You don't need a single perk in either, and it doesn’t max out. Unless you go over 2.4 billion or so (you really won't need over 1500%.. watch).
Pickpocketing can't be boosted like that btw peeps. It maxes. But you can use a trainer > Pickpocket gold back, until level 60 or so depending on if you add perks to pickpocket skill... I do and then use black book to put perks elsewhere. 
One pair of gloves if you have the last Enchanting perk. With such high sneak, Muffle on boots/shoes becomes useless.
With restoration glitch, you can make accessories for followers that make them invisible also. +1000% one handed/+1000% archery rings are great for skipping smithing entirely... or take it to 1000000 wtf ever lol. The fun doesn’t last tho. Break the game = broken game. Nerfed fun. Initially fun, then it isn't. Loses appeal.

author: @matthewherndon6488
text:
35:13 The part where he's talking to Sophie's "ghost" gave me such _specific,_ storytelling related chills. The same chills I get when reading H.P. Lovecraft or scrolling the SCP wiki. I mean, Mark didn't even put that much into it in the video, but it still played out like a movie in my head:

A desperate survivor finally finds the closest thing he's had to a real human being since his arrival on Proteus, only to hear, "Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

He tries again, with a question he's needed to ask for days, one only another human could provide a satisfying answer to: "Who am I??"

"Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

"I have more questions!" he screams.

"Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

Manic hyperventilating gives way to calmer gasps. Finally, the survivor places a hand on the bio bed, and breathes, "Goodbye, Sophie."

And one last time, the monotone voice of the NoA node echoes, "Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."



Not that I want to deprive the world of his let's plays for another 6-10 years, but good god, can you imagine if this man made a Subnautica movie?
video_title: THE HOT ZONE | Subnautica 2 - Part 7

author: @azagorodthenightbringer7686
text: Gotta say, I liked the way Subnautica 1's lore was presented and done in general, even if it was a bit wooden at times and seemed like a DMs first steps into worldbuilding. Below Zero's lore was... weird, to say the least, and missed the mark entirely. But Subnautica 2, even in it's EA state? It's so well done, and the voice actors and actresses are doing a phenomenal job of selling the vibe and general sentiment of weird hopelessness. It really is quite intriguing, and creates such a great atmosphere. I am really looking forward to the full release, and whatever games release in the same year as Subnautica 2 are going to have a real tough uphill battle for GOTY.

author: @samuelsupernova894
text: That liminal version of central park really hit close to home for me. When I was a little kid and my father was in the hospital, when we'd visit, they'd take me across the street to central park to distract me from all the medical trauma that was happening. They probably thought it'd soothe me with all the nature and playgrounds and such. Instead, central park became a place associated with loneliness, helplessness, and being lied to. I still can't go there without feeling uncomfortable. Seeing the footage of parasite eve where it's completely dark and covered in snow, the protagonist alone and small, with the promise of crowds forever just out of reach... I'll admit I felt something. Fascinating how an old low-poly game can do that.

If there are examples that counter my point then please let me know but does this not make anyone else think that the tragedy has kind of skewed people’s image of the towers? author: @Shantara11
text:
“Time Traveller’s Wife” is a story about a person who due to a biological quirk ends up spontaneously traveling to different times with no warning, and his and wife’s attempts to deal with his condition and the inevitability of future, even with the foreknowledge. The book was undergoing final editing during the 9/11, so that author has added one more scene to the story. At one point on an absolutely unremarkable day, the protagonist his wife into the living room and turns on the TV, and in a few minutes you can see the events of 9/11 play out like they did in our history.

I highly recommend the book, the movie adaptation didn’t do it justice!

heres some tips as a professional T106 rat.
- you can fire the recoiless rifles seperatly by chageing the keybind for "Fire from secondary guns"
- the machine guns on the t106 are ranging machine guns, meaning they have the same balistics as the recoiless guns

author: @hernanedilsonvargascaro4958
text:
I have a suggestion: when the main entity is chasing you, try to hide in a cave, but first cook stone to create the typical mine stone so that when you mine, you can place those same cooked stones at the start of the mine. 

Another option: try to find a mod that can record voices and record your voice in a random location, such as a house created specifically for the entity, so it thinks you are there.

Third option: Try creating traps for the entity, such as:
The lava trap: "Create a floor camouflaged with stone images and place lava underneath so that when the entity tries to find you, it will fall into one." This option doesn't seem very effective, as the entity is very intelligent.


Regarding the second suggestion, the best thing to do, once the voice trap has been placed in the house, is to hide where the entity approaches the house and shoot it with a gun; that was the objective of the video (to kill it).

Your videos are great, keep it up, bro. :D
video_title: Something Was Talking to Me…

author: @bonk150
text:
Ingrid is able to perform Sun Octopus by the player!
Similar to unlocking Shin Akuma and Boss M.Bison, you perform her Monoid taunt (back taunt).
Wait a couple of seconds and the Monoid will start bouncing, The moment Ingrid re-appears on the screen, press 2 neutral taunts and a back taunt in quick succession (before she hi-fives the Monoid). She'll absorb the Monoid and turn into her boss mode version. (she also gains max meter and sun stocks)

Sun Octopus is half-circle forward->up-forward with LK+MK and costs 2 bars (412369 in numpad notation)

She also gains 2 new special moves!
Double quarter-circle back + any kick is a forward thrust where she strikes the same pose as her SFA3 win screen art (it also INSTANTLY puts the opponent in burnout)
Double-down + all 3 punches is a full-screen beam that has a slow startup but massive damage


it's a shame this video was made just moments before this was all discovered LOL
video_title: RATE THE SUPER: Street Fighter 6 Vol. 2

text:
Hands down the supers in SF6 know how to be as flashy as possible. Still, the fact that they also put in "Hidden supers" on top of that too shows how much the developers love the hell out of this game too. 

Who knows what Season 4 will bring. I still question the "leak" myself until we see that trailer, but if Supers look this good at the end of Season 3, I shutter to think what the final character of this game will look like.

author: @soul6733
text: Seeiously Max, grow up on Alex. It's an odd story but it's just following the moderately popular trope in japanese stories of "two children who grew under the same roof eventually fall in love". At least the story make it clear that no malicious intent was there and they only fell in love when they were both consenting adults. There are weirdest stuff in the lore of Street Fighter. Heck, Dhalsim canonically had a kid with his wife when she was 17...and he was 30. Yet never heard anyone complaining about it, either because not many care about Dhalsim or because India be like that.

text: I thought Viper's animation was giving off the idea that she was being the David among Goliaths in the context of SF's roster. The point isn't that she baited people to hit her, but that even in her CA, there is the idea that she is a little bit vulnerable, and is leveling the field with her gears and being technical. Doesn't make her seem weak or reckless, but rather emphasizes how she still has control despite being 'weaker'
